description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a wooden cabinet featuring rounded edges and a polished finish. The front panel displays a large, oval-shaped glass window framed in metallic trim, likely serving as a tuning or frequency indicator. Below the window are several tactile knobs and buttons, arranged symmetrically for tuning and volume control, with two larger knobs on either side and a row of smaller buttons in the center. The left side of the radio features a series of horizontal slats, forming a speaker grille, while the right and rear sides are smooth and unadorned except for a power cord emerging from the back. The radio sits low and wide, with a compact, sturdy build typical of mid-20th-century designs. The overall appearance is dominated by warm brown wood tones, accented by cream and metallic details.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a glass tuning window, multiple control knobs, and a slatted speaker grille on one side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass tuning window, metallic trim, control knobs, and central button array.
description_view_1: Right side view displaying the smooth, rounded wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain wooden back panel and the attached power cord.
description_view_3: Left side view highlighting the horizontal slatted speaker grille and the power cord extending outward.
